narrative_design_spec.synopsis: Across a chain of lawless medieval fantasy port towns, scarred elf rogue Brego returns to the smuggling routes after earning the wrath of Vey Marrow, a crime lord whose reach extends through docks, taverns, and customs ledgers alike. Hunted by the chillingly competent bounty hunter Seris Vale, Brego is forced back into the company of his old crew: sharp-tongued smuggler Nessa Quill and steady, dangerous boatman Orun Pike. The story is intimate, dialogue-driven, and eerie, centering on a vanished blackmail ledger, old betrayals, and the uneasy possibility that broken criminals can still become family. It ends in darkness and uncertainty, with one ally missing, one hunter compromised, and hints of a larger power behind the coast's criminal order.

character_data.name: Brego
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: Scarred male elf rogue in his early thirties, lean wiry build, weathered olive skin, sharp gray eyes, uneven ash-blond hair tied back loosely, nicked pointed ears, old knife scar running from brow to cheek, split lower lip, stubbled jaw, layered dark leather coat over worn travel clothes, fingerless gloves, concealed daggers, guarded and tired expression
dialogue_examples: If I'd known this town still remembered my face, I'd have worn someone else's.
You don't keep scars by winning. You keep them by living a little longer than the other fool.
I didn't leave because I stopped caring. I left because staying would've buried all of us.
Every harbor smells like rot after midnight. Makes liars easier to spot.
If there's a clean way through this, no one's told me.

character_data.name: Vey Marrow
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: Middle-aged human woman crime lord, slender build, pale skin, long black hair streaked with silver and pinned neatly, narrow dark eyes, severe high-cheekboned face, thin lips, immaculate dark velvet coat with embroidered cuffs, heavy signet rings, composed and quietly menacing expression
dialogue_examples: People mistake patience for softness right up until it closes around their throat.
I do not enjoy punishment, Brego. I enjoy accounts balancing.
A secret written down stops being a secret and starts becoming property.
You can call it mercy if that helps you swallow it.
Bring me the ledger, and I may decide history has already taken enough from you.

character_data.name: Seris Vale
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: Androgynous human bounty hunter in their thirties, tall athletic build, cool brown skin, pale gray eyes, straight dark hair braided tightly back, clean angular features, no visible scars, fitted brigandine over practical dark travel leathers, gloves, precise posture, unreadable expression
dialogue_examples: Running is useful. It shows me what you value.
Most people beg when I catch them. You calculate. That's rarer.
Your friends are not safer because you lie badly.
I was paid to find you, not to educate you. The conversation is a courtesy.
Competence is a colder thing than cruelty. Cruelty gets distracted.

character_data.name: Nessa Quill
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: Half-elf woman smuggler in her late twenties, slim wiry build, warm brown skin, dark curly hair with shaved sides, amber eyes, crooked grin showing a gold tooth, small scar at the chin, layered patched coat with belts and hidden pockets, ink-stained fingers, skeptical and sharp expression
dialogue_examples: You vanish for three years and walk back in like the tide owes you an apology?
I kept your name out of certain mouths. Don't make me regret the effort.
Crews don't break all at once. They go rotten in the seams.
If I'm still helping you, don't mistake that for forgiveness.
We were better when we were hungry together.

character_data.name: Orun Pike
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: Broad-shouldered human man boatman in his forties, heavyset muscular build, deep brown skin, bald head, thick beard threaded with small metal beads, broken nose, calm dark eyes, weathered hands with faded rope tattoos, heavy wool coat over rough work clothes, dependable but weary expression
dialogue_examples: Sit down before you fall down. Pride heals slower than stitches.
I don't need the full truth. I need the part that'll keep us breathing till morning.
Nessa bites because she cares. I hit people because I care. Same principle.
You left. That's fact. You came back. That's fact too.
Family's just the debt you keep choosing.

location_data.name: Dreg's Landing Wharf
location_data.location_image_prompt: A lawless medieval fantasy harbor with tar-dark wooden piers, leaning warehouses, crooked cranes, stacked cargo nets, rope bridges between dock structures, slick cobblestones, moored smuggler cutters, salt-stained timber, and distant sea walls

location_data.name: The Eel and Lantern, Brackwater
location_data.location_image_prompt: A cramped dockside tavern interior in a medieval fantasy port town, low warped beams, smoke-stained plaster, green bottle-glass windows, scarred wooden tables, curtained alcoves, a long battered bar, hanging brass lanterns, and nautical clutter

location_data.name: Black Ledger Safehouse, Gullhaven
location_data.location_image_prompt: A hidden upper-room safehouse in a medieval fantasy port town, shuttered windows, mismatched chairs, narrow table covered in route maps, lockboxes, bundled ledgers, concealed trapdoor, rough plank floor, crates, spare bedding, and peeling limewash walls

location_data.name: Chapel of the Drowned Saint, Mire's End
location_data.location_image_prompt: An abandoned tide chapel in a medieval fantasy fishing town, salt-eaten stone arches, cracked saint statue, warped pews, guttering prayer candles, votive ribbons made from old netting, barnacled floor tiles, flooded crypt steps, and damp walls marked by the high tide

location_data.name: Red Wake Counting House
location_data.location_image_prompt: A fortified harbor counting house in a medieval fantasy port city, ironbound oak doors, barred windows, shelves of ledgers and sealed scrolls, customs sigils carved into stone, polished counting tables, locked cabinets, and a private audience chamber

scene_data.narrative_summary: Brego slips into Dreg's Landing under a false name, hoping to disappear among smugglers and night cargo, only to find Seris Vale already waiting in the harbor maze. Rather than take the bounty immediately, Seris corners Brego in a quiet exchange and warns that Vey Marrow wants him alive for reasons worse than revenge. The encounter establishes the hunt, the reach of Marrow's network, and Brego's desperate lack of allies, sending him toward the only people on the coast who might still answer his name.

At the Eel and Lantern in Brackwater, Brego reunites with Nessa Quill, who greets him with equal parts fury, relief, and suspicion. Their tense conversation reveals the old crew shattered after a disastrous job tied to Vey Marrow, and that Marrow now believes Brego stole a blackmail ledger during the fallout. Nessa offers temporary shelter and information, but only if Brego helps uncover who truly sold the crew out years ago.

Inside the Black Ledger Safehouse in Gullhaven, Brego meets Orun Pike and is pulled back into the bruised routine of the old crew. Beneath the guarded warmth, Orun makes clear that Brego's disappearance left Nessa and Orun paying Marrow's blood debts for years. A hidden cache from the failed job suggests the ledger was split, not stolen whole, proving someone closer to Brego has been shaping the truth from the beginning.

Brego accepts a private parley with Vey Marrow at the Red Wake Counting House, expecting threats and finding something colder: patience. Marrow explains that the missing ledger contains names, routes, and debts powerful enough to destabilize the entire smuggling chain, and offers Brego amnesty if he returns it. Her careful retelling of the old betrayal contradicts key parts of Nessa and Orun's story, deepening the mystery and forcing Brego to question whether Marrow is lying less than everyone else.

At the abandoned Chapel of the Drowned Saint in Mire's End, Seris corners Brego again but still refuses to finish the hunt. In an unnervingly intimate exchange, Seris reveals the terms of the bounty have shifted: Marrow now wants proof of which of Brego's former companions can still be manipulated. Seris hints that someone close to Brego has been feeding Marrow information for years, but leaves ambiguous whether this is a warning, a manipulation, or the start of a separate agenda.

Back at the safehouse, Brego forces a reckoning with Nessa and Orun, and the scene becomes a painful accounting of the failed job that broke them apart. Nessa admits she hid part of the ledger to keep Marrow from owning every survivor, while Orun confesses he once traded information for time and protection, making both of them traitors in different ways. The confrontation is raw but intimate, clarifying that their bonds were never clean enough to be innocent and never weak enough to disappear.

At Dreg's Landing Wharf, the three former crewmates attempt a quiet escape aboard a cutter, only to discover that every route has been anticipated. Seris arrives first, calm and unreadable, while Marrow's pressure closes in from offscreen through blocked exits, watchers, and bought loyalties. In a dark compromise, Brego entrusts Nessa with the true ledger fragment while he and Orun use false pages as bait; Seris allows the split to happen for opaque reasons, and Nessa disappears into the fog, leaving Brego unable to tell whether he has saved her or been betrayed again.

In the ruined chapel before dawn, Brego and Orun recover Nessa's last hidden packet and find the missing ledger page inside. It implicates not only Vey Marrow but an unnamed patron further up the coastal chain, suggesting the ports are being quietly gathered under a larger hand. Seris's seal is tucked into the bundle with no explanation, Nessa remains missing, and Marrow is still powerful enough to keep hunting; with no closure and no safe harbor left, Brego chooses the next port over peace, setting the stage for a darker continuation.

beat_sheets.source_scene_summary: Brego slips into Dreg's Landing under a false name, hoping to disappear among smugglers and night cargo, only to find Seris Vale already waiting in the harbor maze. Rather than take the bounty immediately, Seris corners Brego in a quiet exchange and warns that Vey Marrow wants him alive for reasons worse than revenge. The encounter establishes the hunt, the reach of Marrow's network, and Brego's desperate lack of allies, sending him toward the only people on the coast who might still answer his name.
beat_sheets.dramatic_question: Can Brego disappear into Dreg's Landing before Vey Marrow's network closes around him, or has he already been found?
beat_sheets.scene_goal: Find cover in Dreg's Landing under a false name and avoid anyone tied to Marrow long enough to disappear into the coast.
beat_sheets.scene_turn: Brego arrives believing the wharf can still hide him, but Seris proves Marrow has already reached this port and forces him toward the old crew he was trying not to face.

beats.purpose: Establish Brego's return, his hunted mindset, and his immediate goal of disappearing into the harbor traffic.
beats.summary: Brego comes ashore at Dreg's Landing using a false name and folds himself into the night cargo traffic. The wharf is familiar in all the wrong ways: same tar, same rot, same smuggler rhythms, but none of it feels like cover anymore.
beats.revelation: Dreg's Landing still looks like the kind of place Brego used to vanish inside, but he no longer trusts it to hold him.
beats.player_goal: Blend in with the dock traffic and secure a path off the open wharf.
beats.choice_prompt: How does Brego try to pass through the wharf unnoticed?
branch_outcomes: He leans on a practiced dockside lie and buys a little social cover.
He keeps to the shadows and side routes, prioritizing stealth over speed.
He walks with the cargo flow in plain sight, testing whether his alias will hold.
beats.exit_state: Brego gets deeper into the harbor, but his concealment already feels thin.

beats.purpose: Seed that the harbor is not neutral ground and that someone expected Brego's arrival.
beats.summary: Small irregularities start to stack up: a shortcut is closed, a laborer repeats Brego's false name too cleanly, and a lantern signal changes just as he shifts direction. Brego tests the maze and realizes he is being guided rather than ignored.
beats.revelation: Someone in Dreg's Landing knew to watch for him and has quietly prepared the ground.
beats.player_goal: Confirm whether he is being watched and shake the unseen net before it closes.
beats.choice_prompt: Which route does Brego test to break surveillance?
branch_outcomes: A warehouse cut-through is already blocked, proving someone planned for haste.
A higher rope-and-crane path reveals a lookout placed where only a smuggler would think to look.
A crowded dock lane produces a staged interruption that confirms he has been marked.
beats.exit_state: Brego is funneled toward a quieter stretch of wharf and becomes certain the trap is deliberate.

beats.purpose: Reveal Seris Vale and demonstrate their control of the encounter.
beats.summary: In a narrow pocket between stacked nets and slick pilings, Seris Vale steps into Brego's path before he can choose knife, bluff, or flight. Their stillness makes the moment worse than a chase: they have been waiting exactly where he would end up.
beats.revelation: Seris already knows Brego's face, his route, and the fact that his false name was never enough.
beats.player_goal: Read Seris's intent before committing to a desperate move.
beats.exit_state: Brego is cornered into conversation instead of violence, which is more unsettling.

beats.purpose: Deliver the core warning that Marrow wants Brego alive and make the hunt feel more dangerous than a simple bounty.
beats.summary: Instead of taking him, Seris explains that Vey Marrow wants Brego alive, intact, and delivered for an accounting that goes beyond revenge. In the clipped exchange that follows, Brego learns that being caught would mean pressure, questions, and old debts dragged into the light.
beats.revelation: Seris is under orders not to kill Brego casually; Marrow believes he is more valuable alive than dead.
beats.player_goal: Learn why Seris is delaying the capture and what Marrow actually wants from him.
beats.choice_prompt: How does Brego answer Seris's warning?
branch_outcomes: He bluffs ignorance, and Seris coolly implies Marrow's patience is worse than fury.
He needles Seris about the bounty, drawing out that this hunt is about value, not vengeance.
He asks directly about Marrow, earning the clearest warning that being taken alive is the real threat.
beats.exit_state: Brego understands that capture would not be quick or clean; it would be deliberate.

beats.purpose: Show the breadth of Marrow's reach and strip Brego of the hope that he can vanish locally.
beats.summary: Seris points out how thoroughly the port has already been tilted against Brego: bought eyes on the lanes, signal changes, records adjusted, exits quietly narrowed. Brego realizes his alias lasted only as long as Marrow's people allowed it to, and no old contact has surfaced to help him.
beats.revelation: Marrow's influence runs through the docks, the signals, and the local paper trail; Brego has no refuge in Dreg's Landing.
beats.player_goal: Find any remaining local opening to disappear before dawn.
beats.exit_state: The harbor stops feeling like cover and starts feeling like a sealed net with Brego already inside it.

beats.purpose: End the scene by redirecting Brego toward his former crew and launching the next scene.
beats.summary: Seris steps aside and gives Brego a narrow chance to leave, suggesting that if anyone on the coast might still answer his real name, it will be the people he left behind. Whether Brego reads it as warning, manipulation, or courtesy, he leaves Dreg's Landing with one ugly conclusion: he cannot hide, so he must go back to the only allies he has left.
beats.revelation: Seris is choosing not to collect the bounty yet, for reasons they do not fully explain.
beats.player_goal: Decide whether to use Seris's opening and where to run next.
beats.choice_prompt: How does Brego frame his next move after Seris lets him go?
branch_outcomes: He accepts the warning at face value and heads for his old crew immediately.
He assumes Seris is manipulating him, but still chooses the old crew because every local refuge is compromised.
He tells himself he is only borrowing old ties, yet commits to seeking Nessa's help.
beats.exit_state: Brego leaves the wharf unable to disappear and committed to finding the only people who might still answer his name.

beat_sheets.source_scene_summary: At the Eel and Lantern in Brackwater, Brego reunites with Nessa Quill, who greets him with equal parts fury, relief, and suspicion. Their tense conversation reveals the old crew shattered after a disastrous job tied to Vey Marrow, and that Marrow now believes Brego stole a blackmail ledger during the fallout. Nessa offers temporary shelter and information, but only if Brego helps uncover who truly sold the crew out years ago.
beat_sheets.dramatic_question: Can Brego survive his reunion with Nessa and turn her anger into a working alliance before Marrow's ledger rumor leaves him with nowhere to hide?
beat_sheets.scene_goal: Convince Nessa to help him and learn why Vey Marrow is hunting him so aggressively.
beat_sheets.scene_turn: Brego goes from isolated and unwelcome to conditionally reaccepted into Nessa's orbit, but only by agreeing to reopen the old betrayal that destroyed their crew.

beats.purpose: Establish the tavern atmosphere, reintroduce Nessa as a loaded part of Brego's past, and give the player control over the tone of the reunion.
beats.summary: Brego enters the Eel and Lantern hoping to disappear into dockside noise, only to spot Nessa Quill already in the room and already aware of him. Before she decides whether his return is a problem or a wound, he chooses how to approach her.
beats.revelation: Nessa is still in Brackwater, and Brego's face is not one she has forgotten.
beats.player_goal: Reach Nessa without letting the room turn into a public confrontation.
beats.choice_prompt: How does Brego open when Nessa notices him?
branch_outcomes: He leads with an apology, softening the first blow but exposing guilt.
He leads with a sardonic quip, keeping pride intact but provoking sharper hostility.
He leads with blunt business, making his need obvious and inviting suspicion.
beats.exit_state: Nessa acknowledges Brego and draws him into a more private corner on her terms.

beats.purpose: Let the reunion land emotionally and show that Nessa's anger is inseparable from relief and old attachment.
beats.summary: In the relative privacy of a curtained alcove, Nessa greets Brego like someone deciding whether to embrace him or cut him. Her words are acidic and unsparing, but underneath them is unmistakable relief that he is alive at all.
beats.revelation: Nessa never fully let Brego's name disappear, even if she never forgave his leaving.
beats.player_goal: Keep Nessa talking instead of letting the meeting collapse into rejection.
beats.exit_state: The conversation shifts from shock at Brego's return to the unresolved damage he left behind.

beats.purpose: Expose the cost of the failed job and make Brego's disappearance an active emotional grievance, not just backstory.
beats.summary: Nessa lays out what followed the job that broke their crew: chaos, scattered loyalties, and consequences that kept grinding long after Brego vanished. Brego can decide how much to admit about Seris's warning and the danger that drove him here, knowing Nessa will hear every omission.
beats.revelation: The old crew did not simply drift apart; it shattered after a disastrous job tied to Vey Marrow, and the survivors carried the fallout for years.
beats.player_goal: Explain his return without giving Nessa a reason to throw him back out into Marrow's reach.
beats.choice_prompt: How much does Brego tell Nessa about Seris and Marrow's current interest in him?
branch_outcomes: Full honesty earns Nessa's respect but heightens her alarm.
A partial truth keeps some control but leaves Nessa less trusting.
Deflection protects Brego's pride but convinces Nessa he is still hiding the worst of it.
beats.exit_state: Nessa understands Brego is not back out of sentiment alone; he is being hunted back into old company.

beats.purpose: Deliver the scene's central external stake by naming the ledger and clarifying why Marrow wants Brego alive.
beats.summary: Nessa finally names the rumor that has been shadowing his return: Vey Marrow believes Brego stole a blackmail ledger during the chaos of the failed job. The hunt stops looking like ordinary revenge and becomes something colder, tied to written names, routes, and debts powerful enough to ruin people.
beats.revelation: Marrow believes Brego stole the missing blackmail ledger during the fallout from the failed job.
beats.player_goal: Convince Nessa he is not the one who took the ledger, or at least learn how he was made to look like the thief.
beats.exit_state: Brego understands that his face now carries danger for anyone who shelters him.

beats.purpose: Pivot the scene from mutual blame toward a shared mystery by introducing the likelihood of betrayal from inside the crew.
beats.summary: As they compare memories of the job, details stop lining up cleanly enough to blame on panic alone. Nessa's anger sharpens into focus: whether Brego stole the ledger or not, someone benefited from Marrow believing he did.
beats.revelation: The crew may have been sold out from the inside rather than merely broken by bad luck and Marrow's retaliation.
beats.player_goal: Find enough common ground with Nessa to stop being only a suspect and become a partner in the question.
beats.exit_state: Suspicion broadens from Brego alone to an old betrayal still hiding among the survivors.

beats.purpose: End the scene with a fragile alliance, a concrete next step, and an emotional bargain that carries into the next scene.
beats.summary: Nessa offers Brego exactly as much mercy as she thinks he has earned: temporary shelter, some information, and a chance to survive the night. The price is clear—he helps her uncover who truly sold the crew out years ago, or he faces Marrow's coast alone.
beats.revelation: Nessa is willing to protect and inform Brego, but only as part of a joint hunt for the real traitor.
beats.player_goal: Secure Nessa's help without surrendering all leverage or pride.
beats.choice_prompt: Does Brego accept Nessa's terms, push back against them, or demand a little more before following her?
branch_outcomes: He accepts outright, building fragile momentum toward reunion with the old crew.
He pushes back, preserving pride but forcing Nessa to set harder boundaries.
He asks for more before agreeing, gaining a little clarity while proving trust will have to be rebuilt piece by piece.
beats.exit_state: Brego leaves the tavern with a conditional ally, promised shelter, and a new objective: uncover the betrayal behind the failed job.

color_scheme.reasoning: The story is coastal, nocturnal, and morally bruised, so I chose a palette of salt-worn parchment, weathered rope, and tarnished brass over bright fantasy hues. Warm bone tones keep dialogue and UI text readable against a dark interface, while the accent leans toward muted amber to evoke lantern light, ledger ink, and old wealth without softening the menace. Disabled and muted states use deeper dockwood grays so they remain legible but recede naturally, fitting the story’s uneasy, criminal atmosphere.
